channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Indian Airlines Resume Flights to Riyadh Amid West Asia Airspace Monitoring
item.description: The Ministry of Civil Aviation (MoCA) has announced that it is closely monitoring the situation in West Asia as Indian airlines resume operations affected by recent geopolitical tensions. Starting fro...

item.title: California Officials Confirm No Verified Threat from Iran Following FBI Memo
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom reassured the public that there is no imminent threat to the state after an FBI memo reported unverified claims of potential drone attacks by Iranian-affiliated actors...

item.title: Former ISIS supporter opens fire at Old Dominion University, kills one and wounds two before being subdued by ROTC students
item.description: On March 12, 2026, a tragic shooting took place at Old Dominion University in Norfolk, Virginia, inside Constant Hall, the business school building. The suspect, identified as Mohamed Bailor Jalloh, a...

item.title: Fast local trains bypass Ambattur, causing extra travel for commuters
item.description: Commuters travelling to Ambattur are facing delays due to fast local trains arriving from Arakkonam and Tirutanni towards Chennai Central, which do not have a platform on the fast line at Ambattur rai...

item.title: Parliament instructs National Treasury to dissolve nine state corporations and merge 42 others
item.description: Kenyan MPs have directed the National Treasury to implement major reforms in the public sector by dissolving nine parastatals and merging 42 others into fewer entities. This directive comes as part of...

item.title: Sydney Biennale 2026 Explores History, Culture and Political Voices Through Art
item.description: The 25th Sydney Biennale, under Emirati artistic director Hoor Al Qasimi, opens this weekend across multiple venues including the Art Gallery of NSW, White Bay Power Station, and regional galleries. D...

item.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei Outlines Priorities and Vows Revenge for Martyrs
item.description: Iran has officially announced Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei as its new Supreme Leader, following the passing of the previous leader. In his first address, broadcasted on state television, Khamenei extend...

item.title: Restructuring of Raila Odinga’s staff triggers emotional responses at Capitol Hill
item.description: Last Wednesday, March 11, a tense and emotional meeting took place at Capitol Hill involving the late Raila Odinga’s long-serving staff. Five months after Raila’s passing, the meeting, convened by his...

item.title: Former Army National Guard member with prior ISIS support conviction kills one, injures two in Old Dominion University campus shooting before dying
item.description: On March 12, 2026, a shooting occurred at Old Dominion University in Norfolk, Virginia, inside Constant Hall, an academic building. The gunman opened fire around 10:45 a.m., resulting in one fatality ...

item.title: Andile Mpisane Expands Career Into DJing
item.description: Durban-born media personality Andile Mpisane has once again diversified his career, now stepping into the world of DJing. Known previously as a musician, footballer, and football club owner, Mpisane a...

item.title: Americans Debate Whether Tipping Is Necessary at Self-Service Restaurants
item.description: Former NFL player J.J. Watt has reignited a national conversation about tipping in self-service restaurants after asking his followers whether he should tip when there is no table service. In the scen...

item.title: Potential Long-Term Impacts of AI Exposure on Young Children's Development
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly present in the lives of young children, with AI-generated content widely available on platforms like YouTube Kids. While AI tools are being used in early c...

item.title: Alberta Court of Appeal Upholds Canmore’s Livability Tax Bylaw, Allowing Higher Taxes on Non-Primary Residences to Proceed
item.description: A recent ruling from the Alberta Court of Appeal has dismissed an appeal in the case Ross v. Canmore, clearing a major legal hurdle for the Town of Canmore’s Livability Tax Program. This initiative fo...

item.title: Court of Appeal Acquits Man Over Alleged Witchcraft Insult to Mother
item.description: In Tanzania, the Court of Appeal has cleared Civilian Deogratious, also known as Kisandu, who was jailed for allegedly calling his biological mother a witch. The seven-year sentence given by the Kaham...

item.title: Cape Town businessman faces court over alleged R4 million bribe to City Manager
item.description: Shaun Roos, a 56-year-old businessman from Cape Town, has appeared in court accused of attempting to bribe the City Manager, Lungelo Mbandazayo, with R4 million. The alleged bribe was intended to rein...

item.title: Five Nigerian Governors Join APC, Party Now Controls 31 States
item.description: For Nigeria, political matter don hot as five governors don recently waka join the ruling All Progressives Congress (APC). Dis movement dey show say APC dey try secure power ahead of the 2027 election...

item.title: Kim Chiu Shares Emotions Ahead of ‘The Alibi’ Primetime Premiere
item.description: Filipina actress Kim Chiu expressed heartfelt emotions as she prepares for the primetime premiere of her latest project, 'The Alibi', scheduled for March 16. The actress, known for her dedication and ...

item.title: British Forces Intercept Iranian Drones at Iraqi Base Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: British forces stationed in Iraq successfully intercepted and shot down two Iranian drones during overnight attacks on the coalition base in Erbil, the capital of the Kurdistan region, according to De...

item.title: Teenager dies in Melbourne car crash during stolen vehicle incident
item.description: A teenage boy has tragically died in a car crash in Melbourne while driving a stolen Skoda. The incident occurred earlier today, shocking the local community. Authorities are investigating the circums...

item.title: NiMet warns Northern Nigerians on increased risk of meningitis during hot season
item.description: NiMet don raise alarm say people wey dey some northern states for Nigeria fit dey more at risk of Cerebrospinal Meningitis (CSM) as weather don favour the spread of the sickness. For a nationwide advi...
